definition: Server useful-life extensions (Microsoft from 4 to 6 years, adding $3.7B annually to operating income) are a one-directional earnings management ratchet. Further extensions face physical obsolescence constraints (GPU cycles 18-30 months) and auditor pushback. The depreciation cliff when current lives expire is mechanically certain. Aggregate Mag-7 impact: ~$8-12B annually.
evidence: Microsoft 10-K: server life extended 4 to 6 years, $3.7B annual operating income impact (~4% of total)
GPU generation cycles of 18-30 months constrain further extensions
One-directional ratchet: no further extension possible beyond 6-7 years
open_questions: Exact timing of depreciation cliff for each hyperscaler
Whether new accounting interpretations could further extend useful lives
